design_philosophy: Strictly Black & White structural UI with heavy, playful neobrutalist borders and sharp shadows. The UI must feel completely uncolored so the user-generated Doodles provide 100% of the vibrancy. Generous spacing, large chunky interactive areas, and bouncy interactions.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Use generous padding: p-4 to p-8 for containers. The wall itself is infinite.
layout_and_spacing.grid_system: The Doodle Wall uses an absolute positioned coordinate system within a pan/zoom container. Calculate random overlapping positions or a structured grid with random transforms (rotate -5deg to +5deg, translate x/y) for a messy, organic feel.

doodle_wall.description: Infinite canvas container taking up 100vw/100vh. Uses react-zoom-pan-pinch for dragging and zooming.
doodle_wall.visuals: Background should have a repeating CSS dot or grid pattern (black dots on white background with 10% opacity) to signify the 'wall'.

add_doodle_button.description: Floating action button (FAB) at the bottom center or top right.
add_doodle_button.visuals: White background, thick 3px black border, heavy black drop shadow (4px 4px 0 0 #000). Hover state: Translate up slightly and increase shadow to 6px 6px. Active state: Translate down and reduce shadow to 0px.

drawing_canvas_modal.description: Popup containing the 350x350 canvas for drawing.
drawing_canvas_modal.visuals: Neobrutalist window style. White background, thick black borders. Includes an 'X' to close, the 350x350 canvas area (with a 2px dashed black border to indicate drawing area), a row of 8 color circles (the ONLY color in the UI), and a 'Put on Wall' button.

doodle_item.description: Individual user doodle on the wall.
doodle_item.visuals: Square image with a subtle white background or transparent background. On hover, apply a z-index boost and slight scale up. On click, expand to a larger view.

interactions_and_motion.hover_states: All buttons use Neobrutalist pop: transform translate-y-[-2px] and larger black shadows.
interactions_and_motion.transitions: Bouncy spring animations (e.g., framer-motion's default spring) for modal entrance and doodle placement.
interactions_and_motion.drawing: Smooth line drawing on HTML5 Canvas using standard stroke styles with 'round' lineCap and lineJoin for a marker feel.

media_and_assets.icons: Use 'lucide-react' for basic B&W icons (Pencil, X, Plus, ZoomIn, ZoomOut, Move).

instructions_to_main_agent: INSTALL_DEPENDENCIES: Install 'react-zoom-pan-pinch' (for the infinite panning wall) and 'framer-motion' (for playful bounce animations). Add Google Fonts 'Fredoka' and 'Nunito' to index.html.

CANVAS_IMPLEMENTATION: Use standard HTML5 `<canvas>` for the drawing board. Fix size to 350x350px. Use standard mouse/touch event listeners (onPointerDown, onPointerMove, onPointerUp) to draw. Set `ctx.lineCap` and `ctx.lineJoin` to 'round'. Brush size is fixed (e.g., 5px).
STATE_MANAGEMENT: Save the doodle as a Base64 string via `canvas.toDataURL('image/png')` to store in MongoDB.

WALL_RENDERING: Wrap the placed doodles in a `<TransformWrapper>` and `<TransformComponent>` from `react-zoom-pan-pinch` to allow infinite panning. Set the TransformComponent width/height to 100vw/100vh.

PLACEMENT_LOGIC: Assign coordinates (X, Y) to each doodle when saving, or calculate a grid position dynamically. Apply random rotation (e.g., `rotate(-4deg)` to `rotate(4deg)`) so it looks hand-placed.
STRICT_MONOCHROME_UI: No colors in buttons, backgrounds, or borders. Everything UI-related is White, Black, or transparent. The ONLY colors allowed are the 8 palette buttons and the user's drawings.

TESTING_ATTRIBUTES: Add 'data-testid' to all buttons and interactive elements (e.g., 'data-testid="add-doodle-button"', 'data-testid="color-picker-red"', 'data-testid="submit-doodle-button"', 'data-testid="doodle-item"').
